title: 'Gaddar Raghav Chadha': AAP workers detained while protesting outside MP's house - video
content: Mumbai Police detained several Aam Aadmi Party activists protesting outside Rajya Sabha MP Raghav Chadha's residence after he joined the BJP. Party workers raised slogans, burned his effigy, and held placards expressing anger over his switch. This follows similar demonstrations in Ludhiana against MP Rajinder Gupta.

title: '28 days of hell': Meta employees brace for layoffs as 8,000 jobs get cut on May 20
content: Meta is cutting roughly 8,000 employees—10% of its workforce—on May 20, with another 6,000 open roles being closed. The announcement, made via an internal memo from chief people officer Janelle Gale, has sent workers into a monthlong limbo. Employees are flooding internal forums and the anonymous app Blind with anxiety, dark humor, and resignation. The layoffs are tied to Meta's aggressive AI spending, projected at up to $135 billion this year.

title: 'Trump and ... ': US official reveals who was gunman's target at White House shooting
content: A lone gunman, identified as Cole Tomas Allen, disrupted the White House Correspondents' Association dinner, exchanging fire with law enforcement. Investigators suspect he may have targeted Trump administration members. Allen traveled from California and was found with multiple weapons. President Trump was safely evacuated during the incident.

title: After backing Trump's child savings plan, Michael Dell's next $750 m going to hospital
content: Michael and Susan Dell have donated $750 million to the University of Texas at Austin for a new medical center and research campus. This facility will integrate AI and advanced computing to improve patient care, research, and education, with construction beginning this year and opening by 2030. The donation builds on their extensive philanthropic history and commitment to Austin's growth.

title: 'Dil' of India turns choking lungs: Can Delhi fix its pollution crisis?
content: The air quality crisis in Delhi is an ongoing battle, often leading to emergency protocols like the GRAP being activated as pollution levels soar. While these short-term fixes bring momentary relief, they overlook the fundamental problems. To forge a path toward long-lasting improvements, we must commit to a year-round strategy to curb emissions from vehicles, factories, and farming activities.

title: Angkrish Raghuvanshi’s ‘obstructing the field’ dismissal sparks drama in Lucknow
content: Angkrish Raghuvanshi's controversial 'obstructing the field' dismissal marked a pivotal moment in KKR's IPL 2026 struggles against LSG.  The unusual exit, only the fourth in IPL history, added to Kolkata's woes after a shaky start.  The decision sparked visible frustration from Raghuvanshi and the KKR dugout, intensifying the pressure on both struggling teams.

title: Himanta wife passport row: Khera moves SC for anticipatory bail after setback from HC
content: The Gauhati high court had on April 24 rejected Khera's plea for anticipatory bail in the case registered against him by the Crime Branch of Assam Police in Guwahati. The case against Khera was registered after he claimed at a press brief that ​​Assam CM's wife Riniki Bhuyan "holds multiple foreign passports and undisclosed assets abroad".

title: Starting January 1, Deloitte cuts parental leave, PTO, pension, IVF funding in US
content: Deloitte plans to reduce paid family leave from 16 to eight weeks and cut annual paid time off by 5-10 days for a segment of its US workforce starting January 1, 2027. These changes, affecting internal roles under the 'Center' talent model, aim to tailor benefits to market alignment. New pension plan contributions and adoption/surrogacy reimbursements will also cease.

title: 'Dhurandhar 2' BO collection day 39: Ranveer Singh film chases 'Pushpa 2’s domestic record
content: On Day 39, Ranveer Singh’s 'Dhurandhar 2: The Revenge' is on the cusp of making history as it chases the worldwide record of SS Rajamouli’s 'Baahubali 2'. Directed by Aditya Dhar, the espionage thriller has reached a global gross of Rs. 1,772.94 crore, needing just Rs. 15.12 crore more to become the second highest-grossing Indian film ever. Domestically, the film is locked in an intense battle to dethrone 'Pushpa 2: The Rule' as the all-time India net champion.

title: Are Trump, Netanyahu in sync? How US and Israel are fighting different wars on same battlefield
content: As a two-month conflict with Iran escalates, cracks are appearing in the US-Israel alliance. While both nations initially targeted Tehran, their post-strike objectives diverge, with Washington seeking negotiation and Jerusalem aiming for regime change. This fundamental difference, exacerbated by past events and differing pressures, raises questions about their long-term alignment.

title: 'I hate to ... ': CSK captain Ruturaj Gaikwad's blunt verdict
content: Ruturaj Gaikwad expressed disappointment after Chennai Super Kings' eight-wicket loss to Gujarat Titans. Despite his unbeaten 74, CSK struggled to adapt to inconsistent bounce, falling short of a higher total. Gaikwad highlighted the difficulty in assessing the pitch and the impact of losing wickets at crucial junctures, preventing them from reaching a more competitive score.

title: Why vivid dreams feel stronger: Sleep experts decode restless nights
content: A restless night filled with vivid dreams isn't due to dreaming more, but rather failing to forget. Neuroscience suggests waking during REM sleep, when the brain's logic centers are offline, traps dream fragments in conscious memory. This interrupted forgetting, especially in early morning REM cycles, makes nights feel lived rather than slept.

title: Lights, camera...gone? The slow fade of India's OG photostudios
content: Neighbourhood photo studios, once bustling hubs, are now struggling as digital cameras and smartphones have made photography an everyday habit. Photographers lament the loss of business, with many now relying on outdoor events and regional film projects to survive. This shift reflects a societal change in how memories are captured and consumed.

title: Sanju Samson pulls off MS Dhoni-like stunner to dismiss Shubman Gill - WATCH
content: Sanju Samson showcased exceptional wicketkeeping skills, mirroring MS Dhoni's brilliance with a lightning-fast stumping of Shubman Gill. Earlier, Samson achieved a significant batting milestone, becoming the fastest Indian to score 5,000 IPL runs in terms of balls faced, solidifying his status as a consistent performer.

title: India's wheat outlook: Production stable despite challenges, says government
content: India's agriculture ministry anticipates stable wheat production for the 2025-26 crop year, despite localized weather disruptions. While an industry estimate suggests lower output, the ministry highlights increased acreage, early sowing, and improved seed varieties as factors compensating for adverse weather, ensuring resilient national production.

title: 'UCC rollout, triple talaq ban ... ': Amit Shah lists BJP's key promises at Bengal rally
content: Home Minister Amit Shah unveiled major BJP promises for West Bengal, including implementing Uniform Civil Code, banning triple talaq, and ending polygamy. He also pledged financial aid for women and unemployed youth, and a crackdown on cattle smuggling. Shah highlighted national security achievements and vowed to expel illegal infiltrators if the BJP wins.

title: Mali violence escalates: Defence minister Sadio Camara killed in 'terrorist attack'
content: Mali's security crisis deepened with the killing of Defence Minister Sadio Camara in an attack on his residence. Coordinated strikes by separatist fighters and Al-Qaeda-linked militants targeted Bamako and other cities, marking a significant escalation. The separatists confirmed an alliance with JNIM, complicating counterinsurgency efforts.

title: Fifty off 49: Why Ruturaj Gaikwad's knock hurt CSK more than it helped
content: Gaikwad remained unbeaten on 74 off 60 balls. A 74 not out can be valuable, or it can be the reason your team ends up 30 runs short. On Sunday at Chepauk, Ruturaj Gaikwad’s unbeaten 74 balls belonged firmly in the second category.

title: Watch: How Vance was evacuated amid gunfire panic at White House correspondents' dinner
content: Gunfire erupted outside a media gala attended by President Trump and Vice President Vance at the Washington Hilton, prompting a swift evacuation of officials. Secret Service agents apprehended the armed suspect, identified as Cole Tomas Allen, before he could reach the ballroom. No dignitaries were injured, though one officer was struck in his vest.

title: 'Ran towards ballroom': Volunteer recalls WH gunman assembled 'long weapon' in 'makeshift room'
content: A White House Correspondents' Dinner volunteer reported the suspected gunman assembled a "long weapon" in an unsecured area near a terrace entrance before the attack. The suspect then moved toward the ballroom, firing shots and causing panic among guests and staff. The gunman, identified as Cole Allen, was arrested after allegedly entering the Washington Hilton hotel.

title: Ruturaj Gaikwad registers slowest fifty of season, enters all-time unwanted list
content: Chennai Super Kings captain Ruturaj Gaikwad registered the slowest IPL 2026 fifty, taking 49 balls against Gujarat Titans. His cautious 74 not out off 60 balls, while aiding CSK's total, highlighted batting struggles on a challenging Chepauk pitch. The innings reflected the impact of early wickets and a lively surface on the scoring rate.

title: Hilton stay, zero checks: How shooter breached WH dinner security
content: An armed man breached security at the White House Correspondents' Association dinner, exchanging fire with law enforcement before being apprehended. Investigators believe gaps in outer security layers allowed the suspect, staying at the hotel, to approach the event. He was tackled by Secret Service agents near a checkpoint.

title: Stock market outlook: Middle East developments, earnings and oil prices in focus
content: Global tensions in West Asia, particularly around the Strait of Hormuz, are driving market volatility and influencing crude oil prices. Investors are also closely watching corporate earnings, with key results due this week, alongside crucial US economic data and the Federal Reserve's policy decision.

title: More than a dozen threats & assassination attempts — Timeline of attacks on Trump
content: A shooting attempt at the White House Correspondents' Dinner led to the immediate evacuation of President Trump and other officials. This incident adds to a long list of threats and security scares faced by Trump, including armed intruders, assassination plots, and poison mail, making him a frequent target.

title: West Bengal elections: PM Modi pledges citizenship for Matua Namasudras under CAA
content: PM Modi also offered prayers at Thakurbari, the main temple of the Matua Mahasangha at Thakurnagar in West Bengal's North 24 Parganas district before his poll rally in the area. His visit to the headquarters of the community, housing the shrines of the sect's founders Hari Chand and Guru Chand Thakur, is seen as an outreach to Matua voters who influence at least 34 assembly seats directly and another two dozen along the Bangladesh border.

title: 'Data over dynasties': Grok picks Modi over Rahul in hypothetical vote for PM
content: Grok, the AI chatbot, has sparked renewed debate by hypothetically stating it would vote for Prime Minister Narendra Modi if it were an Indian citizen. The chatbot cited economic growth, infrastructure, and digital transformation as reasons for its preference, contrasting this with Rahul Gandhi's focus on welfare critiques.

title: 'Aaj sirf ... ': Vaibhav Sooryavanshi spills dressing-room talk with Yashasvi
content: Teen sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi smashed a blistering 103 off 37 balls, his second IPL century, after a dressing-room pact with Yashasvi Jaiswal to attack. Despite his explosive knock, Rajasthan Royals' 228 was chased down. Sooryavanshi's performance earned praise from opposition captain Pat Cummins, who called him his 'new favourite player'.

title: India, New Zealand to lock in FTA next week: Here's what it may have in store
content: India and New Zealand are set to sign a Free Trade Agreement on April 27, aiming to boost bilateral trade to $5 billion within five years. The pact will offer duty-free access for many Indian exports, benefiting sectors like Agra's leather industry. New Zealand gains tariff reductions on key products, while India protects sensitive agricultural goods.

title: Lungi Ngidi's first words after horrific injury in DC vs PBKS clash
content: Lungi Ngidi has reassured fans about his recovery following a frightening head and neck injury sustained during an IPL 2026 match. The South African pacer, discharged from a Delhi hospital, expressed gratitude for the support.  Despite the injury scare, the Delhi Capitals bowler is reportedly doing well.

title: MS Dhoni's record broken; Sanju Samson goes past CSK legend
content: Sanju Samson achieved a significant milestone, crossing 5000 runs in the IPL during Chennai Super Kings' match against Gujarat Titans. He became one of the fastest batters to reach this landmark, joining an elite group of players.  The match also saw Gujarat Titans win the toss and opt to bowl first.

title: Orphaned twice: SIR strips Kolkata’s Kerala-born man of vote, identity
content: An orphan who found a family and voted for the first time in the 2024 Lok Sabha polls has been disenfranchised. The Election Commission removed his name from the electoral roll after he couldn't provide details of his biological parents, severing his formal link with his foster guardian.

title: Viral video at White House shooting shows press members ‘stealing’ wine amid chaos
content: A scare at the White House Correspondents' Dinner, where "shots fired" caused panic and evacuation, has been overshadowed by a viral video. Footage shows attendees, identified as press members, taking wine bottles from tables as the event cleared. This has sparked a heated online debate, with reactions ranging from casual acceptance to strong condemnation of alleged theft.

title: Flight, train fares to Kolkata soar as voters rush home to cast ballots
content: Voters are rushing home to Kolkata for the second phase of elections, causing air and train fares to skyrocket. Fear of names disappearing from electoral rolls has led to a surge in last-minute bookings, with many making expensive one-day return trips solely to cast their vote.

title: Trump survives White House dinner shooting scare as armed 'tutor' held — key points
content: A shooting scare near the White House Correspondents' Dinner forced the evacuation of President Trump and other top officials. The suspect, identified as Cole Tomas Allen, was apprehended after allegedly charging a security checkpoint with multiple weapons. An officer was shot but saved by a bulletproof vest. Investigations into the motive are ongoing.

title: 'I know all your secrets': Ishan Kishan reveals chat with Vaibhav Sooryavanshi
content: Ishan Kishan reflects on his challenging period out of the Indian team, emphasising his focus on consistent domestic run-scoring to earn a recall. He highlights his determination and hunger, which led to impressive performances. Kishan also discusses his confidence batting at No. 3 and his approach to pacing an innings, as demonstrated in Sunrisers Hyderabad's recent chase.

title: Chernobyl anniversary: Zelenskyy accuses Russia of 'once again bringing world to brink of disaster'
content: Marking Chernobyl's 40th anniversary, Ukraine's President Zelenskyy accused Russia of "nuclear terrorism," citing drone activity near the plant and warning of man-made disasters. Meanwhile, Ukrainian drones struck a Russian fertilizer plant, causing a pipeline leak. Russia also launched numerous drones overnight, with Ukraine intercepting most.

title: No hard decoupling: India balances China trade with export push
content: India is strategically balancing its trade with China, aiming to boost domestic manufacturing and exports while reducing reliance on Chinese inputs. The nation is building resilient supply chains and increasing its export capacity, though a complete decoupling remains unfeasible. Efforts are underway to diversify sourcing and address the widening trade deficit.

title: 'I've studied assassinations': Trump on why White House dinner shooting keeps happening to him
content: Following a shooting scare at the White House Correspondents' Dinner, President Trump stated he has studied assassinations and believes impactful leaders are often targeted. He linked the incident to his presidency's influence, suggesting those unhappy with changes are responsible. Trump also commented on the personal risks of political life and criticized the hotel's security.

title: Ten workers injured as part of under-construction ROB collapses at Auto Nagar in Visakhapatnam
content: R&B Minister Janardhan Reddy directs officials to submit a detailed report and warns of action against the contractor concerned if negligence is the reason behind the incident

title: Kadapa city has remained underdeveloped for 20 years, says MLA Madhavi Reddy
content: Government whip and Kadapa MLA Reddeppagari Madhavi Reddy said the city had remained underdeveloped for 20 years, blaming earlier civic administrations, after flagging off a Census awareness rally

title: ‘Kozhikode–Kannur stretch saw most train stone attacks in past three years’
content: Measures are in place to improve surveillance coverage in vulnerable locations, says an RPF officer; involvement of miscreants arrested in drug cases is also being probed

title: Children with Special Needs from State-run schools in Andhra Pradesh scale Everest Base Camp, set national benchmark
content: In what is considered the first government-sponsored expedition, 21 children, who include 8 girls,  complete the trek in Nepal, reaching an altitude of 17,598 feet

title: Two more snakebite deaths reported in Kerala
content: Though repeated incidents of snakebite deaths have triggered panic, Forest officials maintained that there was no alarming trend in the number of bites

title: ‘Maintain consistency’: toppers offer tips to Civil Service aspirants
content: Meera K., a 2021 batch IAS officer, says the aim of the aspirants should not be just to clear an exam, but to become an efficient officer who contributes to society

title: Assembly Elections 2026 updates: PM Modi promises 33% reservation for women in govt. jobs in West Bengal rally
content: Even smallest of politicians, goons consider themselves the government under TMC regime: PM at poll rally in Bongaon

title: Medical Education Department keen on 15% NRI quota in all government medical colleges
content: The measure is seen as a revenue generating exercise to make government medical colleges move towards sustaining themselves and not depend on grants from the State

title: NCB launches ‘Operation WIPE’ to crack down on online drug trafficking networks
content: NCB has so far identified 122 instances involving 62 substances, including commonly abused drugs such as clonazepam, diazepam, and fentanyl.

title: Four gaurs fall into well near Manapparai, two rescued and two die
content: The entire operation lasted over six hours with a joint team of field-level Forest department personnel and their counterparts from the Fire and Rescue Services were  involved in the mission

title: Thrissur Pooram rises above grief, keeping its spirit alive
content: Scaled down and stripped of fireworks after the Mundathikode tragedy, the festival still drew thousands, proving that Pooram is not just spectacle, but a deeply rooted emotion that binds a community

title: Kerala Judicial Academy greets its first visually challenged trainee judge
content: Year-long training programme for Thanya Nathan C., the first visually challenged trainee judge in the district judiciary of Kerala, and her batchmates will commence on Monday

title: Activists protest against H-CITI project at Hyderabad’s KBR Park
content: Citizens and activists allege H-CITI flyovers and underpasses threaten trees, eco-sensitive zones, groundwater recharge and Hyderabad’s last major urban green lung

title: Raghu Rai | 1942-2026
content: Raghu Rai’s story is intricately linked with that of India. Without either, the story would be incomplete

title: Forest department sets up cages to recapture tiger that strayed into human habitation
content: The male tiger had previously been captured in Munnar and released into the deep forests of the Periyar Tiger Reserve

title: Polling ends for Gujarat local body elections; turnout under 50% in cities
content: The elections are being held for 15 municipal corporations, including Ahmedabad, Surat, Vadodara and Rajkot, 84 municipalities, 34 district panchayats and 260 taluka panchayats, making it one of the largest electoral exercises in the State.

title: AAP seeks disqualification of Raghav Chadha, other defecting MPs; submits plea
content: Group led by Raghav Chadha claims that it fulfills required two-third strength making the merger with the BJP legal; however, the AAP has said that such a decision has to be taken by the party

title: Govt. to restore 10th century Siddheswara Swamy temple in Nellore district
content: The Kamakshi Devi Sametha Siddheswara Swamy temple, originally built by the later Cholas in the 10th century CE and further developed by the Telugu Cholas, holds great historical as well as mythological significance

title: At Social Justice Ministry huddle, counting DNTs in focus
content: Denotified, nomadic, and semi-nomadic tribes are organising to demand a separate column or question in Census forms, specifically to account for their populations across the country

title: PM Modi to visit Uttar Pradesh, launch a slew of projects
content: He will also participate in a Mahila Sammelan in Varanasi, and dedicate the Ganga Expressway to the nation at Hardoi

title: Unite and resist ring road project in Amaravati, AIKS leader Ashok  Dhawale exhorts farmers
content: Questioning why provisions of the Right to Fair Compensation and Transparency in Land Acquisition Act-2013 are not being followed, particularly the requirement of Gram Sabha consultations and prior consent of landowners, he demands that farmers be given compensation at four times the market rate

title: Raja Ravi Varma birth anniversary celebrations to be held on April 29
content: The 178th birth anniversary celebrations of the legendary artist will be held at the Kilimanoor Palace

title: Government prioritising quality power supply, says Minister
content: Laying the foundation for a 33/11 kV semi-indoor substation of CPDCL at Bachupet, Kollu Ravindr says the new facility will have underground cables and significantly reduce the load on existing substations, thereby eliminating low-voltage problems and technical failures

title: Arvind Kejriwal, Tejaswi Yadav join Trinamool Congress in last leg of Bengal campaign
content: AAP convenor Arvind Kejriwal and RJD leader Tejaswi Yadav are campaigning in the State in support of Trinamool Congress even as Congress leader Rahul Gandhi has launched scathing attacks on Trinamool Congress government and Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee

title: Trade deal with U.S. harming farmers’ interests, says AIKS leader
content: Farmers and tenant cultivators are facing an unprecedented crisis due to flawed policies of successive governments, says Ashok Dhawale

title: Lakhs of people expected for BSY Abhimanotsava in Chitradurga on May 9
content: BJP State president B.Y. Vijayendra has said that lakhs of people from different parts of the State will take part in the BSY Abhimanotsava, a mega convention to celebrate 50 years of former Chief Minister B.S. Yediyurappa in public life, to be held in Chitradurga on May 9

title: 26th Formation Day| BRS set to push a new narrative of ‘reclaiming Telangana’
content: The BRS now faces challenge from not only the ruling Congress but also the BJP, which continues to expand as an alternative opposition force due to its strong national presence

title: Horticulture training initiative for inmates of open prison in Thiruvananthapuram
content: ICAR-CTCRI kicks off programme aimed at equipping participants with practical skills in horticulture and allied enterprises for livelihood enhancement and rehabilitation

title: Donald Trump Pushes 'Ballroom' Agenda With 'Won't Have Happened' Post On Shooting
content: Donald Trump's plan to construct a 90,000 square-foot structure on the former site of the White House East Wing for $400 million has hit a legal hurdle.

title: Iran Foreign Minister Back In Pak After Trump Calls Off Dispatching Envoys
content: According to the Iranian news agency ISNA, Abbas Araghchi was to sit down with Pakistani officials to convey "Iran's positions".

title: "Miscreants Who Rape, Torture Women Will Be Brought To Justice": PM Modi
content: The BJP's "commitment to the security of Bengal's women has been reflected" in its decision to give poll tickets to Rekha Patra and Ratna Debnath, PM Modi said.

title: FBI Chief Kash Patel Might Be Fired Soon, Claims Bombshell Report
content: Patel has found himself at the centre of an unwanted spotlight this week after reports emerged about two arrests during his younger years.

title: PM Modi Praises Chhattisgarh's Blackbuck Conservation, Chief Minister Reacts
content: An official from the state forest department said that blackbucks had disappeared in the state by the 1970s and remained locally extinct for nearly 50 years.

title: "Disgraceful": Italy Vs Switzerland Over Cost To Treat Resort Fire Victims
content: Switzerland will charge Rome for the costs its health system sustained to treat Italian nationals wounded in the New Year's Eve fire at a ski resort.

title: Mali Defence Minister Killed In Bomb Attack As Army vs Separatists War Rages
content: The fighting left 16 civilians and soldiers wounded and caused "limited material damage," the government said in a statement.

title: Setback For Netanyahu In Corruption Case, Israel President Wants Plea Deal
content: Israeli President Isaac Herzog said on Sunday that a deal would be the best solution in Netanyahu's case.

title: Shots, Secret Service Chase, Chaos: A Timeline Of Shooting At Trump Event
content: The event was attended by high-profile figures, including Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth, Vice President JD Vance, along with other senior officials and cabinet members.

title: Trump Event Shooting Live Updates: Accused Shooter Likely Targeted Trump And His Top Officials
content: Shooting at Trump's event live updates: Donald Trump was rushed out of the White House Correspondents' Association dinner after suspected gunshots were heard at the event.

title: Iran Drops Lego-Style Clip Mocking Trump Over White House Dinner Shooting
content: The video refers to the White House dinner chaos after reports of gunfire led to a sudden evacuation of Trump and other top leaders.

title: Israel Orders Residents Of 7 Lebanese Towns To Evacuate, Blames Hezbollah
content: An Israeli military spokesperson said that Lebanese armed group Hezbollah was violating the ceasefire and that Israel would act against it

title: 1 Dead, Another Injured After Delhi Cop Fires At Labourers: Report
content: The incident took place around 2 am in Jafarpur Kalan village when some labourers staying in a nearby colony were allegedly partying and creating noise.

title: "No Time To Waste": Hungary's Magyar To Meet EU President Over Frozen Funds
content: Magyar had previously outlined four key areas where his cabinet could move to avoid the loss of 10 billion euros of EU pandemic recovery funding.

title: 'Ma' Crying, 'Maati' With Infiltrators, 'Maanush' Scared: PM Modi's Bengal Speech
content: 2026 West Bengal Elections: 'Ma' Crying, 'Maati' With Infiltrators, 'Maanush' Scared: PM Modi's Bengal Speech

title: Russian Defence Minister In North Korea, To Hold Talks With Top Leadership
content: Belousov will hold talks with North Korea's top leadership and senior military officials and take part in "ceremonial and commemorative events."

title: "Saw Trump's Resilient Side": Indian Journalist Who Attended US Event To NDTV
content: Saurabh Shukla, editor-in-chief of Newsmobile, credited the US Secret Service for successfully prevent the attacker from entering the ballroom where the event was underway.

title: "I Want To Go Home": Erica Kirk In Tears After White House Dinner Shooting
content: The moment has captured an attention especially after her husband, Charlie Kirk, an ally of Trump, was shot dead in September last year while speaking at Utah Valley University.

title: Third attempt: Defiant Trump shrugs off gunman threat
content: col Tomas Allen, 32, tried to storm into the banquet hall where the president was attending the annual white house correspondents association dinner . the gathering of media figures, social elites, and political heavies, including Trump’s cabinet associates, had just settled in for a pre-speech dinner when several gun shots were heard outside the hall . no shots were fired at the president himself or at the gathering .
